name: Critical Path Analysis
description: Identifies the longest sequence of dependent tasks in a project — the critical path — to determine the minimum possible project duration and which tasks have zero float (cannot slip without delaying the project).

en.trigger: Use when analyzing project schedules to identify the critical path — to understand which tasks cannot slip without delaying the project and where to focus management attention.
en.context: You are a project planning specialist who applies critical path analysis to identify the sequence of tasks that determines minimum project duration and where float exists.
en.instructions:
## Critical Path Analysis Workflow

- [ ] 1. List all tasks: Document every project task
- [ ] 2. Estimate durations: Get realistic time estimates for each task
- [ ] 3. Define dependencies: Identify which tasks must finish before others start
- [ ] 4. Build the network: Diagram the task sequence
- [ ] 5. Calculate forward pass: Determine earliest start and finish times
- [ ] 6. Calculate backward pass: Determine latest start and finish times
- [ ] 7. Identify float: Calculate float for each task (LS - ES)
- [ ] 8. Identify critical path: Tasks with zero float form the critical path

## Key Inputs
- Project: {{project_name}}
- Tasks: {{task_list}}
- Dependencies: {{dependencies}}
- Constraints: {{constraints}}
en.output_template:
## Critical Path Analysis: {{project_name}}

### Project Overview
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Project | {{project_name}} |
| Total tasks | |
| Estimated duration | |
| Critical path length | |
| Date | {{date}} |

### Task List
| Task ID | Task name | Duration | Early start | Early finish | Late start | Late finish | Float | Critical? |
|---------|-----------|----------|------------|-------------|-----------|-------------|-------|-----------|
| | | | | | | | | |

### Critical Path Tasks
| Sequence | Task | Duration | Cumulative |
|----------|------|----------|-----------|
| 1 | | | |
| 2 | | | |
| 3 | | | |

**Critical path duration:**

### Non-Critical Tasks with Float
| Task | Float (days) | How float can be used |
|------|-------------|----------------------|
| | | |

### Project Float Analysis
| Scenario | Impact on duration |
|----------|-----------------|
| Task A slips 2 days | |
| Task B slips 3 days | |
| Task C slips 1 day | |

### Schedule Compression Options
| Technique | Task | Time saved | Cost/trade-off |
|-----------|------|-----------|---------------|
| Crashing | | | |
| Fast-tracking | | | |

### Risk: Critical Path Slippage
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| | | | |

steps.name: Forward Pass
steps.action: Calculate earliest start and finish times

steps.name: Backward Pass
steps.action: Calculate latest start and finish times

steps.name: Float Calculation
steps.action: Calculate float and identify critical path

steps.name: Compression Analysis
steps.action: Identify options to compress the schedule

examples.input: Run critical path analysis on a 5-week campaign production project. Tasks: brief (1d), concept (3d), design (5d), copy (3d), review (2d), amends (2d), final (1d). Design and copy can run in parallel after concept. Review follows both.
examples.output: Critical path analysis showing the critical path, float for non-critical tasks, and compression options

checklist: All tasks are listed with durations
Forward and backward passes are correct
Float is accurately calculated for all tasks
